text: Sprint 5 check-in to review KPI progress, surface remaining stability issues, and formalize the go/no-go decision process. Main concerns: tail-latency spikes during burst tests, KV cache warm-up time, and SSO/KMS validation. Team agreed on an A/B canary starting with 5% traffic and gating on 99th percentile latency, error rate, and token-cost budget. Action owners assigned and a decision meeting scheduled.

Agreed actions: Redwood to provide sampling scripts + runbook; Velaris to provide traffic profile and initial SLO preferences; schedule bench run in staging.

Topics covered:
- Definitions: TTFT (time to first token) vs TPOT (total per-token time), measurement windows, warm cache vs cold cache behavior.
- Target setting: p95 / p99 latency targets and accepta...

text: Tabletop exercise to rehearse OOM scenarios against Dedicated pools and Hosted fallbacks ahead of the planned model rollout. We ran through simulated failure modes, prioritization for mitigation playbooks, and assigned short-term operational runbooks and longer-term infra tasks. Key outcome: sequence of tactical checkpoints (stress tests, improved observability, updated canary abort criteria) and explicit owners for each contingency step.

text: Deep technical triage on asymmetric KV-cache priming issues that trigger divergent CUDA-graph replay paths and kernel selection changes. Security and on-prem constraints discussed; next steps include experiments on Helio dev cluster and sharing low-level perf traces.

Follow-up demo/benchmark readout scheduled next week.
